item.title: FASANOC | Team Fiji arrives in Glasgow
item.description: The largest ‘wave’ of Team Fiji to the 2026 Commonwealth Games departed Nadi on Sunday morning for the long two days of travel to Glasgow. The 29-member team, comprised of Chef de Mission, Ajay Ballu, team manager Jane Wong-Niubalau, Basketball, Bowls and Swimming teams together with Dr Wong and two physiotherapists of the medical 1team.
item.content:encoded:
The largest ‘wave’ of Team Fiji to the 2026 Commonwealth Games departed Nadi on Sunday morning for the long two days of travel to Glasgow.

The 29-member team, comprised of Chef de Mission, Ajay Ballu, team manager Jane Wong-Niubalau, Basketball, Bowls and Swimming teams together with Dr Wong and two physiotherapists of the medical 1team.

Travel was via Melbourne/Dubai where swimmer Anahira McCutcheon joined the team, whilst fellow team mate Tolu Young met the team in Glasgow after a four day training camp in Europe enroute from his college in the USA.

Team Fiji’s Jane Wong-Niubalau confirmed the teams arrival on Monday night, Scotland time, with the majority of the team in one ‘village’ while the swimming team is at another location closer to the competition venue.

The weightlifting team departed Nadi on Tuesday morning while the remaining athletics team and a physio will leave on Wednesday morning. The team will travel via Melbourne where Yeshnil Karan and Rusiate Matai will join the team. Both athletes have been training in Brisbane and Melbourne for the last few weeks of final preparation.

Judoka Gerard Takayawa, based in Japan will fly to Manchester on July 23, for a five day camp with UK based coach Mike Liptrot before joining the team in Glasgow on July 29.

The boxing team have had a 10 day training camp in Ireland with coach Cam Todd and met up with the main team in Glasgow on Monday. Para athlete Sitiveni Konataci and SnC Coach Fesaitu Mario having been based in the UK and Scotland for a GAPS Camp will meet up with the main athletics team in Glasgow on Thursday.

Travel is staggered as sports can only check into the Games villages four days prior to competition. Accommodation is spread over three hotels for the Games, rather than the traditional Games Village, given Glasgow stepped in only two years ago when Victoria, Australia withdrew from hosting the Games.

Excitement is building within the team who are looking forward to the opening ceremony that will be held on Thursday July 23.

Competition is scheduled to commence on July 24, however due to the large number of bowlers entered, their competition may start a day before.

Technical meetings will be held 1-2 days before competition where draws and schedules will be finalised.

Wishing Team Fiji all the very best for the 2026 edition of the Commonwealth Games where 3000 athletes from 74 nations and territories will be competing in 10 sports.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Drua locked for decider
item.description: FIJI set to host historic Super Rugby W Grand Final with and unchanged squad in the McDonald’s Fiji Fijian Drua Women side for the title decider against NSW Waratahs at the Four R Stadium in Ba this Saturday. Head coach Mike Legge has named an unchanged Match-Day 23 for the Grand Final.
item.content:encoded:
FIJI set to host historic Super Rugby W Grand Final with and unchanged squad in the McDonald’s Fiji Fijian Drua Women side for the title decider against NSW Waratahs at the Four R Stadium in Ba this Saturday.

Head coach Mike Legge has named an unchanged Match-Day 23 for the Grand Final.

The same squad that secured last week’s Semi-Final victory will now carry the hopes of a nation into the biggest match in the club’s history.

The most capped Drua player Karalaini Naisewa starts out at loosehead prop, with Tiana Robanakadavu at tighthead and Keleni Marawa starts at hooker.

Mereoni Nakesa starts at lock position partnering with Carletta Yee. Nunia Daunimoala continues at blindside flanker, while Salaseini Railumu takes her place on the openside. Sulita Waisega starts the match again at number 8.

In the backline, Kolora Lomani starts at halfback and will also captain the side on field with Varanisese Qoro who is at flyhalf.

Aqela Raitubu starts on the left wing with Atelaite Buna on the right wing.

The speedster Josivini Naihamu starts at inside centre with Merewairita Neivoha at outside centre. Litiana Vueti completes the starting line-up at fullback.

For impact this week, seasoned captain Bitila Tawake along with Zipporah Sorokacika, Keri Lawavou, Karavaki Lutumaibau, Alfreda Fisher, Evivi Senikarivi, Repeka Tove and Verenaisi Ditavutu that will complete matchday squad.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: A boost for Fiji Bati World Cup hopes
item.description: THE Fiji Bati could enter this year’s Rugby League World Cup with a major boost in their squad depth, with dual-code star Mark Nawaqanitawase and Wests Tigers trio Latu, Samuela and Sione Fainu among the players being considered for the tournament. Fiji Bati head coach Mick Potter has already submitted a preliminary 40-man squad to the Fiji National Rugby League board as preparations continue, wit
item.content:encoded:
THE Fiji Bati could enter this year’s Rugby League World Cup with a major boost in their squad depth, with dual-code star Mark Nawaqanitawase and Wests Tigers trio Latu, Samuela and Sione Fainu among the players being considered for the tournament.

Fiji Bati head coach Mick Potter has already submitted a preliminary 40-man squad to the Fiji National Rugby League board as preparations continue, with the inclusion of several high-profile players expected to strengthen Fiji’s chances on the world stage.

The Fainu brothers remain one of biggest selection stories, with the Wests Tigers trio awaiting final approval to switch their international allegiances to Fiji through their grandmother’s Fijian heritage.

Speaking to NRL.com, Potter confirmed officials are working through the required documentation but remains hopeful the brothers will be cleared.

“There’s three players at Wests Tigers who grandmother is Fijian, and that’s the Fainu boys,” Potter told NRL.com.

“We’re trying to get that over the line. They’re just trying to get the documentation right.”

Potter believes the inclusion of the brothers would provide valuable experience and strength to the Bati squad.

“I’m very hopeful. They’d be great players to be playing for Fiji,” he said.

Nawaqanitawase is another major name being considered, with Potter believing the Sydney Roosters flyer could have a significant impact if he chooses to represent Fiji.

“I think he’d be way better to play for Fiji, wouldn’t he,” Potter said.

The Bati coach believes Nawaqanitawase’s involvement could create a major boost for rugby league in Fiji, with his profile capable of attracting greater interest in the sport.

“It would send it to another level if we went back to Fiji and he was there. The floodgates of rugby league would open up there.”

Nawaqanitawase brings valuable representative experience, having been part of the victorious New South Wales State of Origin team and the Kangaroos squad that toured England for the Ashes series last year.

If he commits to Fiji, he could line up alongside established Bati stars including Viliame Kikau, Api Koroisau, Sunia Turuva, Jahream Bula and Miaka Sivo.

The Bati will begin their World Cup campaign against the Cook Islands on October 18 before facing Australia and New Zealand in Pool A, with Potter aiming to guide Fiji back to the semi-finals after three consecutive appearances from 2008 to 2017.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Nasova lift Ba rugby campaign
item.description: FRESH from his Super Rugby season winger, Joji Nasova will provide a major boost to the Vinod Patel Ba Rugby side ahead of the opening Skipper Cup round next Saturday in Suva. The Men in Black will open their campaign against traditional rivals Suva at HFC Bank Stadium in Suva, with expectations high following the completion of their local club grand final competition last weekend.
item.content:encoded:
FRESH from his Super Rugby season winger, Joji Nasova will provide a major boost to the Vinod Patel Ba Rugby side ahead of the opening Skipper Cup round next Saturday in Suva.

The Men in Black will open their campaign against traditional rivals Suva at HFC Bank Stadium in Suva, with expectations high following the completion of their local club grand final competition last weekend.

Head coach Ben Tora and his management team have selected a balanced squad comprising experienced players and exciting young talents from various districts around Ba.

The side boasts depth in the forwards with the inclusion of players such as Allen Osborne, Ratu Peni Dekedeke, Sirieli Qauqau and powerful No.8 Lekima Vereyali, while the backs feature exciting talents including Josese Bola, Joseva Nokalevu and fullback Tevita Nailati.

Ba Rugby officials believe the squad has the potential to challenge for top honours this season, with the players eager to restore the union’s status as one of Fiji’s rugby powerhouses.

The team management includes head coach Ben Tora, assistant coach Tuifagalele Naliva, strength and conditioning coach Sanaila Vunisa, manager Pita Matakitoga and assistant manager John Sadole.

Loose Head Props: Sevuloni Qata (Sasa), Filipe Tuitubou (Duiyasana), Jordan Mamao (Sorokoba), Noa Tora (Toge).

Hookers: Gideon Cagimaivei (Rarawai FSC), Peniasi Sokia (Toge), Meli Cabe Jr (Duiyasana).

Tight Head Props: Allen Osborne (Sorokoba), Emosi Kautoga (Natalecake), Sekove Tuvakaikoya (Rarawai FSC), Jone Naqiri (Toge), Pita Rasila (Services Ba).

Locks: Saimone Tagitagivalu (Nailaga), Waisale Senimoli (Sorokoba), Josefa Roiroi (Sorokoba), Arimela Noa (Toge).

Blindside Flankers: Ratu Peni Dekedeke (Natalecake), Nemani Qaravatu (Toge), Peceli Weleliakeba (Rarawai FSC).

Openside Flankers: Sirieli Qauqau (Rarawai FSC), Peni Nasova (Toge), Sakiusa Tuwai (Koronubu).

No.8: Lekima Vereyali (Navala), Gabby Naliva (Sorokoba).

Halfbacks: William Ryland (Toge), Bruce Sautulevu (Rarawai FSC), Sailasa Takali (Ba River), Vatukilagi Ratu (Natalecake).

Second Five-Eighths: Viliame Cama (Ba River), Valami Kunavue (Natalecake).

Flyhalves: Joseva Nokalevu (Vatutuvi), Josaia Naisua (Ba River), Josese Bola (Services Ba).

Outside Centres: Panapasa Nalatu (Nailaga), Peni Waqa (Toge), Damasio Levi (Navala).

Wingers: Meli Tuvusa (Natalecake), Iliavi Masori (Toge), Joji Nasova (Toge), Jerry Matai (Lomavata), Waisale Naturaga (Yakete), Solomoni Druku (Nailaga), Epeli Draunidalo (Ba River).

Fullbacks: Tevita Nailati (Natutu), Josateki Vale (Nailaga).

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: EPL race continues this week
item.description: The race for the 2026 Extra Premier League title is tightening, with just two points separating the top three teams heading into Round 15 this weekend. League leaders Ba FC sit on 30 points, narrowly ahead of Labasa FC (29) and Rewa FC (28), ensuring every result could have a major impact on the championship race.
item.content:encoded:
The race for the 2026 Extra Premier League title is tightening, with just two points separating the top three teams heading into Round 15 this weekend.

League leaders Ba FC sit on 30 points, narrowly ahead of Labasa FC (29) and Rewa FC (28), ensuring every result could have a major impact on the championship race.

Ba will have another stern examination when they host fifth-placed Lautoka at 4R Stadium, Govind Park this Sunday.

The Men in Black edged Navua 1-0 last weekend to reclaim top spot, but the Blues will be eager to bounce back after suffering a 1-2 home defeat to Nasinu.

Second-placed Labasa travel to face Navua in what shapes as another crucial encounter.

The Babasiga Lions were held to a 2-2 draw by Suva in Round 14 and cannot afford another slip-up with Ba and Rewa breathing down their necks.

Rewa, meanwhile, head into their clash against Nadi full of confidence after a commanding 6-0 demolition of Nadroga.

The Delta Tigers boast the league’s second-best goal difference and will be targeting another three points to keep pace with the leaders.

Elsewhere, fourth-placed Nasinu travel to Lawaqa Park to meet Nadroga, while Suva host bottom-placed Tailevu Naitasiri at HFC Bank Stadium.

Nasinu have emerged as one of the competition’s surprise packages and will be looking to strengthen their hold on a top-four finish, while Suva will see their fixture as an opportunity to climb from sixth place.

With the title race heating up and valuable points on offer at both ends of the table, Round 15 promises another weekend of high-stakes football across the country.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: New gym boosts fitness across Suva-Nausori corridor
item.description: SPORTS and fitness enthusiasts in Nasinu and the Suva-Nausori corridor now have access to a brand-new, state-of-the-art gym. Grace Roads Group officially opened their two-million-dollar True Fitness centre at the True Mart complex in Laqere.
item.content:encoded:
SPORTS and fitness enthusiasts in Nasinu and the Suva-Nausori corridor now have access to a brand-new, state-of-the-art gym.

Grace Roads Group officially opened their two-million-dollar True Fitness centre at the True Mart complex in Laqere.

This impressive gym will be the fourth largest in the country, covering 1000 square meters and equipped with the latest training gear suitable for all fitness levels.

The gym also boasts a male and female locker rooms with each room providing 50 lockers and 3 showers for members and teams using the facility.

Grace Roads president Daniel Kim said fitness and giving back to the community are key values for the organization.

“We want to build a strong, healthy community, and fitness plays a big part in that. That’s why every development we do includes a gym,” he said.

As part of their commitment to the community, the Nasinu Rugby and Volleyball teams will be able to train at the facility for free.

President Kim said this isn’t the first time they’ve supported local sports.

“At our Navua sports facility, we also offer free use to the Namosi Rugby team, and they’ve used it many times to prepare for their games,” he shared.

He added that in the future, support will extend beyond rugby and volleyball to include all sports clubs in the areas where Grace Roads operates, helping to encourage sports and fitness for everyone.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Simpson ready for her debut
item.description: GLADNESS Simpson is preparing to take the next major step in her athletics journey as she gets ready to represent Fiji at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland. The Mahatma Gandhi Memorial School athlete has quickly established herself as one of Fiji’s promising young talents, following a series of impressive performances at national and regional levels.
item.content:encoded:
GLADNESS Simpson is preparing to take the next major step in her athletics journey as she gets ready to represent Fiji at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland.

The Mahatma Gandhi Memorial School athlete has quickly established herself as one of Fiji’s promising young talents, following a series of impressive performances at national and regional levels.

Simpson announced herself on the domestic stage with a standout performance at the 2025 Coca-Cola Games, where she was named the overall best female athlete after winning gold medals in the Senior Girls’ 800 metres, 1500 metres and 4×400 metres relay events.

Her achievements earned her selection to represent Fiji at the 2025 Pacific Mini Games in Palau, where she competed in the 800m and 1500m events, gaining valuable experience on the regional stage.

Now preparing for her first Commonwealth Games appearance, Simpson said the training program has been demanding, but the support from her training partners has helped make the process more enjoyable.

“It’s pretty tough compared to previous preparations, but I have good training friends, and they make it easier and fun for us,” Simpson said.

For Simpson, the opportunity to wear the Fiji colours on the Commonwealth stage is a proud moment and one she is eager to embrace.

She said representing Fiji internationally has always been part of her ambitions, and she is focused on producing her best performance in Glasgow.

“I’m just going to do my best at the Commonwealth and try to achieve anything.”

Beyond the games, Simpson hopes to continue developing as an athlete and explore where her athletics journey can take her.

With a strong foundation built through school athletics and regional competition, Simpson now looks forward to testing herself against some of the best athletes in the Commonwealth while proudly representing Fiji on the international stage.

The Commonwealth Games are scheduled to take place from Friday to August 2.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys hunt for final berth, WC qualification
item.description: AFTER finishing top of Group A, the Junior Bula Boys head into today’s semi-finals OFC Under-16 Men’s Championship semi-final against Solomon Islands with confidence, but they know the biggest challenge of their campaign awaits. The Junior Bula Boys have impressed throughout the tournament with their discipline, determination and ability to handle pressure. However, the semi-finals stage brings a
item.content:encoded:
AFTER finishing top of Group A, the Junior Bula Boys head into today’s semi-finals OFC Under-16 Men’s Championship semi-final against Solomon Islands with confidence, but they know the biggest challenge of their campaign awaits.

The Junior Bula Boys have impressed throughout the tournament with their discipline, determination and ability to handle pressure. However, the semi-finals stage brings a different level of intensity, where small moments can decide who moves one step closer to the title.

Leading the charge of Fiji has been Rewa’s Isoa Baselala, who has played a key role in guiding the team through its successful group-stage campaign.

Baselala has shown strong leadership on and off the field while also making his impact count when it matters most.

He scored in Fiji’s final group stage against Papua New Guinea.

His composure and ability to step up in important moments will be vital as the Junior Bula Boys prepare for a tough semi-final against Solomon Islands.

Despite the team’s strong run so far, head coach Sunil Kumar believes there is still room for improvement as Fiji enters the knockout stage.

“For us, it’s one step at a time, and I can see that the boys are getting confidence, growing in the game. Still, there are a lot of areas for us to improve,” Kumar said.

Kumar’s focus remains on keeping the players grounded while building on the progress made throughout the tournament.

With a place in the final at stake, the Junior Bula Boys will need to maintain their discipline, composure and belief when they face Solomon Islands.

Kick-off is set for 6pm today, as the Junior Bula Boys look to continue their impressive campaign after finishing top of Group A.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.
